_meta.note: Daemon-backed scoped rule deactivation. scope is REQUIRED: it is present in the MCP input schema required[] (TB-5), so an omitted scope is rejected at the wire-form boundary, never silently widened to global. This fixture uses explicit global scope. TB-6: a deactivate that matches no active (rule_id, version, scope) row is NOT a silent ok; it returns a typed RuleNotActive teaching error that echoes the actually-active version(s).

rule_not_active_error_example.message: no active row for rule 'rust-compile-error' v99 scope global; active version is 1

invariants: exactly ONE selector of {rule_id, rule_ids, pack} is required; rule_id is schema-optional and the adapter's exactly-one-of validator owns required-ness (US2/FR-011)
version is optional and only valid with rule_id; bulk selectors close each rule's active version(s) under the scope
scope is required and present in the MCP input schema required[] (TB-5)
an omitted scope is rejected, never silently widened to global
jobs_rebound is a count, not job output
single-rule success payload reports whether an active binding was closed
a single-rule deactivate matching no active (rule_id, version, scope) row returns a typed RuleNotActive error, not a silent ok:true/was_deactivated:false
the RuleNotActive message echoes the version(s) actually active under the scope so the caller can self-correct
bulk (rule_ids/pack) reports one outcome per requested rule in request order: deactivated | not_active | unknown_rule; partial success is explicit, never silent (FR-011)
pack membership resolves from the embedded seed-pack JSON only, never id-prefix or tag heuristics; an unknown pack lists the known packs
bulk jobs_rebound reflects ONE post-loop rebind pass, not one per rule
